title: Rhea Seehorn Wins First Golden Globe for 'Pluribus'
text: Actress Rhea Seehorn claimed her first Golden Globe Award, winning Best Female Actor in a Drama Series for her performance in Vince Gilligan's sci-fi series "Pluribus." The win came as a surprise to Seehorn, who joked upon taking the stage that her "stomach is still over there" at her table. Seehorn acknowledged her fellow nominees, including Bella Ramsey, Britt Lower, Helen Mirren, Kathy Bates, and Keri Russell. She expressed gratitude for the support of women in the industry, noting that the experience had been meaningful to her. The actress specifically thanked Gilligan, saying: "for writing me the role of a lifetime" and praised his belief in her talent. She concluded by expressing her ongoing appreciation for her career in the entertainment industry. This Golden Globe marks Seehorn's second major award for the role, following a Critics Choice Award win the previous week. While she has received Emmy and Critics' Choice nominations throughout her career, this represents her first Golden Globe nomination and win. Seehorn previously collaborated with Gilligan on the critically acclaimed AMC series "Better Call Saul," which despite 54 Emmy nominations, never secured a win during its six-season run.
